reasoning: Article 5 of the NATO treaty has only been invoked once in its history (after the 9/11 attacks). Searching news through the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025 confirms it has not been invoked so far in 2025. In September 2025, a significant Russian drone incursion into Poland resulted in Poland invoking Article 4 (for consultation), not Article 5. This demonstrates a high threshold for escalation even in the face of cross-border incidents from the Ukraine war. With only about seven weeks remaining in the year, the likelihood of a major, deliberate military attack against a NATO member that crosses the threshold for an Article 5 collective defense invocation is extremely low. Thus, the probability of this event occurring before December 31, 2025 is near zero. I am setting it at the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.
